question.text: Which of the following categories of entities is NOT eligible to apply for registration as a KRA with SEBI?

answer.text: A wholly owned subsidiary of a recognized stock exchange having a nation-wide network of trading terminals
A wholly owned subsidiary of a Self-Regulatory Organization (SRO) registered under SEBI (SRO) Regulations, 2004
A wholly owned subsidiary of a depository or any other intermediary registered with SEBI
A partnership firm registered under the Indian Partnership Act

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI KRA Regulations, 2011, only three categories are eligible for KRA registration: (a) wholly owned subsidiary of a recognized stock exchange with a nation-wide network, (b) wholly owned subsidiary of a depository or other SEBI-registered intermediary, or (c) wholly owned subsidiary of an SRO registered under SEBI (SRO) Regulations, 2004. A partnership firm does not fall under any of these categories. **The Trap:** Candidates may assume any SEBI-registered entity can apply for KRA registration, but only specific wholly owned subsidiaries are eligible. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The emphasis on 'wholly owned subsidiary' ensures that KRAs have strong institutional backing and governance frameworks.
